subject: Accounting
title: Cost of Goods Sold
description: A short lesson to help you apply Cost of Goods Sold.

hook.text: A bakery sells a cake for $30. The flour, eggs, and butter cost $10. That $10 is COGS.

buildup.text: Cost of goods sold captures the direct costs of making the products a company sells.

discovery.text: Subtract COGS from revenue and you get gross profit—the first measure of profitability.

twist.text: A company with high revenue but higher COGS is losing money on every sale it makes.

climax.text: Managing COGS is critical—it determines whether the core business model works at all.

punchline.text: If making it costs more than selling it, you're done.

quiz.question: What does COGS measure?
options: Direct costs of producing goods sold
